title: funfo vs Kantan Chumon: Free POS Testing vs Published Modular Pricing
metaTitle: funfo vs Kantan Chumon: Restaurant POS and Mobile Ordering Compared (2026)
description: Compare funfo and Kantan Chumon by published pricing, free testing, POS and mobile ordering, multilingual service, customer marketing, subsidies, and implementation model.
lead: funfo is an app-led restaurant platform that can be tested from a zero-fee tier. Kantan Chumon publishes monthly prices for POS, handheld, mobile-order, LINE-order, and tablet-order configurations. Compare testability, required hardware, implementation support, and total cost separately.
author: Luqevora.com Editorial Team

sections.heading: Conclusion: choose funfo for no-fee self-testing and Kantan Chumon for a quoted modular deployment
body: funfo publishes a zero-fee Free tier and lets a restaurant test the iPad and smartphone-order workflow directly. It fits operators that want to validate one location before moving to a paid tier.

Kantan Chumon publishes a JPY 2,980 monthly POS-only starting point and separate configurations for handheld and ordering workflows. It suits restaurants that want to select a named configuration and confirm hardware, payment, installation, and support in a deployment quotation.

sections.heading: Key differences
body: Compare the implementation model rather than treating both products as identical POS packages.

rows: Tablet POS and customer smartphone ordering
LINE acquisition and advanced customer functions
Recommendation and repeat-order functions described in partner materials
Japanese, Chinese, English, and Korean supported

sections.heading: Cost and rollout
body: With funfo, add the iPad, printers, payment fees, support, and the paid tier needed for concurrent devices or customer management.

Kantan Chumon publishes monthly tax-inclusive prices of JPY 2,980 for POS only, JPY 8,980 for POS plus handheld, JPY 8,980 for web mobile ordering, JPY 12,980 for LINE mobile ordering, and JPY 15,980 for TTO ordering. Confirm hardware, payment fees, installation, extra devices, support, and contract terms separately.

sections.heading: Operations and customer growth
body: funfo is differentiated by the visible LINE and customer-management path, especially in the higher tier. It is worth testing when the restaurant wants ordering data to support repeat-customer communication.
Kantan Chumon emphasizes smartphone ordering, sales analysis, and recommendation-related functions. Confirm exact plan availability and reporting screens in a demonstration.

sections.heading: Which should a restaurant choose?
body: Use the restaurant’s implementation capability and commercial requirements.
bullets: Choose funfo to test at zero monthly fee, use an existing iPad, and roll out in stages
Consider Kantan Chumon for guided requirements definition and subsidy discussion
Compare both for high-order-frequency formats such as izakaya, yakiniku, cafés, and casual dining

sections.heading: Common evaluation checklist
body: Send both providers the same requirement sheet.
bullets: Menu modifiers, courses, and table movement
KPIs: waiting time, average check, table turns, and order errors

faqs.question: Which product is cheaper?
faqs.answer: funfo has a zero-fee starting tier. Kantan Chumon publishes a JPY 2,980 monthly POS-only plan and higher-priced configurations. Compare total cost including hardware and payment processing.

faqs.question: Which is easier to test?
faqs.answer: A restaurant with an iPad can test funfo directly. Kantan Chumon is evaluated through inquiry, demonstration, and quotation.

faqs.question: Which is better for subsidies?
faqs.answer: A restaurant with an iPad can test funfo directly. For Kantan Chumon, review the published plan first and then test the selected POS, handheld, or ordering configuration through a demonstration and quotation.
